title: Parabolic avalanche scaling in the synchronization of cortical cell assemblies
authors: Capek, E.; Ribeiro, T.L.; Kells, P.; Srinivasan, K.; Miller, S.R.; Geist, E.; Victor, M.; Vakili, A.; Pajevic, S.; Chialvo, D.R.; Plenz, D.
key_finding: 2-photon imaging of awake mouse superficial cortex shows synchronized cell assemblies organize as scale-invariant avalanches with quadratic temporal-profile scaling chi=2 over up to 5s and 1 mm^2 (Nat. Commun. 14:2979)
hypothesis_generated: Measure shape-collapse with chi=2 expectation on LLM activation avalanches; quadratic profile is a sharper test than exponent fits alone

title: Critical scaling of novelty in the cortex
authors: Vakili, A.; Ribeiro, T.L.; Kells, P.; Capek, E.; Plenz, D.
key_finding: Targeted holographic stimulation of single pyramidal neurons in awake mouse V1 produces power-law recruitment with slope 0.2-0.3 embedded in ongoing critical avalanche activity (Nat. Commun. 16 art.68277) VERIFY DOI
hypothesis_generated: Novel-token perturbations in LLMs may recruit downstream units with power-law distributions if the net is critical; gives a perturbation protocol analog to ROME/patching

title: Sleep restores an optimal computational regime in cortical networks
authors: Xu, Y.; Schneider, A.; Wessel, R.; Hengen, K.B.
key_finding: 10-14 day continuous recording in freely behaving rats: waking progressively disrupts criticality; sleep restores it; deviation predicts future sleep/wake (Nat. Neurosci. 27:328-338) VERIFY pages
hypothesis_generated: Training-inference asymmetry in LLMs (Candidate 19) has a direct biological analogue; use deviation-from-sigma=1 as a 'fatigue' metric across training steps

title: Scale-free behavioral dynamics directly linked with scale-free cortical dynamics
authors: Jones, S.A.; Barfield, J.H.; Norman, V.K.; Shew, W.L.
key_finding: Mouse V1 2-photon + behavioural tracking: scale-free behaviour and scale-free neural activity are linked but scale-free activity is restricted to a winner-take-all subset of neurons (eLife 12:e79950)
hypothesis_generated: Sparse-subset criticality motivates basis-dependent analysis (Candidate 5): SAE features vs raw neurons; the critical subset may be hidden in raw activations

title: Low-dimensional criticality embedded in high-dimensional awake brain dynamics
authors: Fontenele, A.J.; Sooter, J.S.; Norman, V.K.; Gautam, S.H.; Shew, W.L.
key_finding: Awake mouse motor cortex: low-dim subspace shows large fluctuations consistent with criticality; orthogonal high-dim subspace is desynchronised; critical signature appears only at long timescales (Sci. Adv. 10:eadj9303)
hypothesis_generated: Criticality in LLM activations may live in a low-dim subspace (e.g. top-k SAE features) while orthogonal subspace looks desynchronised; search for subspace-resolved sigma

title: Is criticality a unified setpoint of brain function?
authors: Hengen, K.B.; Shew, W.L.
key_finding: Meta-analysis of 140 cortical-criticality datasets 2003-2024; argues criticality is homeostatic setpoint unifying cognition and disease (Neuron 113:2582-2598)
hypothesis_generated: Mandatory review to cite as up-to-date critical-brain-hypothesis framing; provides numerical baseline of cortical exponents and dataset count

title: Defining and measuring proximity to criticality
authors: Sooter, J.S.; Fontenele, A.J.; Barreiro, A.K.; Ly, C.; Hengen, K.B.; Shew, W.L.
key_finding: Temporal renormalization group and information-theoretic distance-to-criticality for observed time series without known control parameter (bioRxiv 2025.08.03.668332)
hypothesis_generated: Use temporal-RG estimator on LLM activation time series as a fourth criticality observable alongside alpha sigma and Lyapunov rho

title: Tackling the subsampling problem to infer collective properties from limited data
authors: Levina, A.; Priesemann, V.; Zierenberg, J.
key_finding: Comprehensive review of subsampling biases and correction methods across neural avalanche networks and disease-spread systems (Nat. Rev. Phys. 4:770-784)
hypothesis_generated: Mandatory methodology reference; LLM-activation subsampling by layer/head/feature must use these bias-correction schemes

title: Consciousness is supported by near-critical slow cortical electrodynamics
authors: Toker, D.; Pappas, I.; Lendner, J.D.; Frohlich, J.; Mateos, D.M.; Muthukumaraswamy, S.; Carhart-Harris, R.; Paff, M.; Vespa, P.M.; Monti, M.M.; Sommer, F.T.; Knight, R.T.; D'Esposito, M.
key_finding: Modified 0-1 chaos test on human+macaque ECoG/MEG: low-freq cortical oscillations near edge-of-chaos during wakefulness; transition away during anaesthesia/seizure; psychedelics move closer to critical point (PNAS 119:e2024455119)
hypothesis_generated: Direct empirical edge-of-chaos framework for cortex; replicate 0-1 chaos test on LLM residual-stream time series as an independent criticality observable

title: Criticality supports cross-frequency cortical-thalamic information transfer during conscious states
authors: Toker, D.; Muller, E.; Lendner, J.D.; Mishne, G.; Pappas, I.; Paff, M.; Monti, M.M.; Sommer, F.T.; Knight, R.T.; D'Esposito, M.
key_finding: Concurrent thalamic-cortical recordings across species: 1-13 Hz to 52-104 Hz cross-frequency transfer peaks when cortex is near critical point; disrupted under anaesthesia (eLife 12:RP86547)
hypothesis_generated: Cross-frequency transfer analog in transformers: low-freq positional vs high-freq token-level patterns; if critical init supports transfer this predicts training dynamics

title: Genetic contributions to brain criticality and its relationship with human cognitive functions
authors: Xie, Z.; et al. VERIFY author list
key_finding: HCP twin rs-fMRI + Allen Brain transcriptomics: brain criticality metrics are substantially heritable; criticality of primary sensory cortex more genetically determined than association cortex; critical state correlates with total cognition (PNAS 122:e2417010122)
hypothesis_generated: Establishes criticality as heritable biological phenotype; argument for criticality being a deep computational principle worth seeking in LLMs

title: Critical dynamics predicts cognitive performance and provides a common framework for heterogeneous mechanisms impacting cognition
authors: Muller, J.; Miron, G.; et al. VERIFY
key_finding: Multi-day invasive EEG of 104 epilepsy patients + cognitive battery: long-range temporal correlations predict cognitive performance; three mechanisms (IEDs, ASMs, SWA) push system subcritical (PNAS 122:e2417117122)
hypothesis_generated: Clinical validation that deviation from criticality degrades information-processing; ML analogue: deviation from sigma=1 predicts benchmark score

title: The cortical critical power law balances energy and information in an optimal fashion
authors: VERIFY authors
key_finding: Fisher-information + metabolic-cost analysis proves the experimentally observed cortical critical power-law exponent is the unique energy-information Pareto optimum for population coding (PNAS 122:e2418218122)
hypothesis_generated: Energy-information optimality argument transfers to LLMs: test whether critical exponents of activations minimise FLOPs-per-bit-of-mutual-information

title: Quasiuniversal scaling in mouse-brain neuronal activity stems from edge-of-instability critical dynamics
authors: Morales, G.B.; di Santo, S.; Munoz, M.A.
key_finding: Mouse-brain Neuropixels data matches Landau-Ginzburg edge-of-instability model predictions; quasi-universal power-law exponents emerge from edge-of-synchronisation not classical DP (PNAS 120:e2208998120)
hypothesis_generated: Replaces pure-DP expectation with edge-of-instability framework; LLMs may sit at edge-of-instability of residual-stream synchronisation instead of DP critical point

title: Statistical modeling of adaptive neural networks explains co-existence of avalanches and oscillations in resting human brain
authors: Lombardi, F.; Pepic, S.; Shriki, O.; Tkacik, G.; De Martino, D.
key_finding: Adaptive Ising-class model with two free parameters fits MEG avalanche + oscillation coexistence; gamma exponent ~1.6 rather than critical-branching's 2; argues sub-critical adaptive regime suffices (Nat. Comput. Sci. 3:254-263)
hypothesis_generated: Alternative null model: LLM activations may be sub-critical-with-adaptation rather than at DP; add adaptive-Ising fit as competitor to Clauset-Shalizi-Newman

title: Emergence of a Dynamical State of Coherent Bursting with Power-Law Distributed Avalanches from Collective Stochastic Dynamics of Adaptive Neurons
authors: Cocchi, L.; Lombardi, F.; Tkacik, G.; De Martino, D. VERIFY
key_finding: Power-law avalanches emerge from stochastic input + adaptation balance; neither critical branching nor DP required (PRX Life 3:013013)
hypothesis_generated: Another non-critical-null: power-law avalanches from stochastic bursting; must be excluded before claiming criticality in LLMs

title: Transition to chaos separates learning regimes and relates to measure of consciousness in recurrent neural networks
authors: Mastrovito, D.; Liu, Y.H.; Kusmierz, L.; Shea-Brown, E.; Koch, C.; Mihalas, S.
key_finding: Training pushes both ordered and chaotic RNNs toward edge of chaos; transition aligned with perturbational complexity index PCIst used clinically for consciousness (bioRxiv 2024.05.15.594236)
hypothesis_generated: Direct precedent that training trajectories converge to edge-of-chaos (Candidate 3); also bridges ML training to clinical consciousness metric

title: Dynamical stability and chaos in artificial neural network trajectories along training
authors: Danovski, K.; Soriano, M.C.; Lacasa, L.
key_finding: Treats training as dynamical system in graph space; computes Lyapunov exponents of network trajectories; finds regular vs chaotic regimes depending on learning rate (Front. Complex Syst. 2:1367957)
hypothesis_generated: Lyapunov-of-training as opposed to Lyapunov-of-forward-pass is a complementary observable for Candidate 3 and Candidate 15 (early-stop signal)

title: Lyapunov spectra of chaotic recurrent neural networks
authors: Engelken, R.; Wolf, F.; Abbott, L.F.
key_finding: Complete Lyapunov spectrum of chaotic RNNs; extensive chaos with size-invariant spectrum; attractor dimension much smaller than phase space; symplectic-like point symmetry (Phys. Rev. Research 5:043044)
hypothesis_generated: Gold-standard methodology for computing Jacobian-spectrum Lyapunov on transformer residual streams; use for per-layer EOC measurement

title: Geometric Dynamics of Signal Propagation Predict Trainability of Transformers
authors: Haber, A.; Levi, O.; VERIFY author list
key_finding: Order-chaos phase transition in transformers as function of init hyperparameters; derives angle-exponent + gradient-exponent Lyapunov indices; attractive/repulsive particle regimes (Phys. Rev. E 110:044309 or equivalent; VERIFY)
hypothesis_generated: Transformer-specific edge-of-chaos theory; direct quantitative prediction to validate on pretrained GPT-2 / Pythia

title: Universal Scaling Laws of Absorbing Phase Transitions in Artificial Deep Neural Networks
authors: Yoneda, K.; Nishimori, H.; Hukushima, K. VERIFY authors
key_finding: MLPs belong to mean-field universality class; CNNs belong to directed-percolation class; hyperparam tuning to phase boundary necessary but insufficient for optimal generalisation (Phys. Rev. Research jp61-6sp2)
hypothesis_generated: First direct assignment of artificial NN architectures to DP universality classes; test same for transformers; motivates class-assignment in Candidate 20

title: Critical Avalanches in Excitation-Inhibition Balanced Networks Reconcile Response Reliability with Sensitivity for Optimal Neural Representation
authors: Yang, Z.; Liang, J.; Zhou, C.
key_finding: Heterogeneous inhibition in E/I-balanced networks produces reliable critical avalanches simultaneously maximising response sensitivity and reliability (Phys. Rev. Lett. 134:028401)
hypothesis_generated: E/I heterogeneity analog in transformers: attention-head gain heterogeneity may control reliability-sensitivity tradeoff at criticality

title: Intelligence at the Edge of Chaos
authors: Zhang, S.; Patel, K.; VERIFY author list
key_finding: Training GPT-2 on elementary cellular automata of varying complexity: downstream reasoning peaks for ECAs near edge of chaos; uniform and fully chaotic ECAs produce weaker models (ICLR 2025)
hypothesis_generated: Training-data complexity edge-of-chaos hypothesis: ML systems trained on edge-of-chaos distributions become smarter; complementary to Candidate 1 measuring criticality-in-activations

title: Improving Sparse Decomposition of Language Model Activations with Gated Sparse Autoencoders
authors: Rajamanoharan, S.; Conmy, A.; Smith, L.; Lieberum, T.; Varma, V.; Kramar, J.; Shah, R.; Nanda, N.
key_finding: Gated SAE separates which-directions from magnitude-estimation; Pareto improvement over vanilla SAE for interpretability metrics (NeurIPS 2024)
hypothesis_generated: Updated SAE basis to use for Candidate 5 basis-invariance test; Gated SAE features may give cleaner criticality signature than vanilla SAE

title: Dynamical constraints on neural population activity
authors: Oby, E.R.; Degenhart, A.D.; Grigsby, E.M.; Motiwala, A.; McClain, N.T.; Marino, P.J.; Solla, S.A.; Yu, B.M.; Batista, A.P.
key_finding: Monkey motor cortex BCI: neural populations cannot violate natural timescales of activity even under direct behavioural challenge (Nat. Neurosci. 28:383-393) VERIFY pages
hypothesis_generated: Activations in trained nets may be similarly constrained to a low-dim manifold; criticality may live on this manifold not in raw ambient space

title: Sensory-Processing Sensitivity Is Associated with Increased Neural Entropy
authors: VERIFY authors
key_finding: Resting-state EEG analysis combining DFA + neuronal-avalanche analysis + sample entropy + Higuchi fractal dim reveals entropy increases with SPS trait (Entropy 25:890)
hypothesis_generated: Example of standard criticality toolbox applied to human data; mirrors the ML-activation pipeline we propose

title: Is critical brain dynamics more prevalent than previously thought?
authors: VERIFY authors
key_finding: Neural systems may separate non-critical dynamics critical oscillations and scale-invariant avalanches into different subspaces; hidden criticality widely underdetected (bioRxiv 2025.09.02.673722)
hypothesis_generated: Subspace-hidden criticality hypothesis; for ML supports testing criticality in carefully chosen subspaces (SAE features, top-PC, residual channels) not ambient activations

title: Different developmental trajectories of critical dynamics in prefrontal cortex-amygdala circuitry
authors: VERIFY authors
key_finding: Developmental trajectory of prefrontal avalanche branching ratio decreases toward 1 with age; amygdala follows distinct trajectory (bioRxiv 2026.02.13.705704)
hypothesis_generated: Developmental analogue for training dynamics (Candidate 3); different 'regions' of a transformer (early/late layers, FFN vs attention) may follow distinct trajectories

title: Critical neuronal avalanches emerge from excitation-inhibition balanced spontaneous activity
authors: VERIFY authors
key_finding: Spiking model with E/I balance reproduces critical exponents observed in calcium + electrophysiology data without fine tuning (bioRxiv 2025.11.17.688775)
hypothesis_generated: Alternative generative model for LLM-activation avalanches; compare fit quality against DP branching and adaptive Ising

title: Invariant activity sequences across the mouse brain
authors: VERIFY authors
key_finding: Allen Brain Neuropixels data: invariant activity sequences preserved across brain regions; statistics consistent with critical propagation (bioRxiv 2025.12.20.695676)
hypothesis_generated: Cross-region universality in brain analog to cross-layer universality in LLMs; test invariance of avalanche shape across transformer layers (Candidate 6)

title: How neural network structure alters the brain's self-organized criticality
authors: VERIFY authors
key_finding: Graph-structure perturbations shift SOC regime; hub removal and small-world structure qualitatively alter avalanche exponents (bioRxiv 2024.09.24.614702)
hypothesis_generated: Direct precedent for Candidate 2 skip-connection-topology-tunes-criticality thesis; biological equivalent of topology sweep
